PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Mar-21-2022 Text Ruling

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-21-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Shalaby, via zoom, for Jesus and Rito Quinonez, via zoom, Petitioners; Stephen Bucher, via zoom, for Guadalupe Martin and Manuela Quinonez, Petitioners; Janet Boessenecker, via zoom, for the SF Public Administrator ("SFPA"), Interested Party.

The court queries counsel regarding whether the parties have agreed to a third party neutral. Mr. Shalaby and Mr. Bucher offer oral testimony in support of their individual positions. Mr. Bucher reports that he is amenable to a third-party neutral being appointed while Mr. Shalaby requests that his clients be appointed and states that an evidentiary hearing be set. The court suggests that it would be wise for the parties to agree to a neutral to reduce expenses to the estate. After additional conversation, Ms. Boessenecker advises that the SFPA is ready to serve as Administrator if appointed. The court advises that pursuant to Ms. Boessenecker's testimony, the court will be asking the SFPA to formally file a petition to be appointed. Jesus Quinonez privately discusses the court's tentative ruling with Rito Quinonez, returns and advises that he has confirmed that Rito agrees to have the SFPA appointed. As the parties have agreed to the appointment of the SFPA, the court advises Ms. Boessenecker to submit a proposed order thereon.

After additional discussion, both matters on today's calendar are submitted.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Teebay. Not reported.

***AFTER HEARING: Upon further consideration, the will dated Sep-06-2020 is being admitted to probate as of the date of the hearing, Mar-21-2022.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Feb-28-2022 Continued To Mar-21-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-28-2022 Continued To Mar-21-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Shalaby, via ZOOM, for Rito Quinonez and Jesus Quinonez, Petitioners; Steve Buscher, in person, for Manuela Quinonez, in court, and Guadalupe Martin, via ZOOM, Petitioners; Janet Boessenecker, via ZOOM, Observing.

The court advises the parties that it is not inclined to appoint either of the petitioners as Co-Administrators and suggests that the parties meet and confer regarding agreeing to appointing a third party neutral to avoid costly litigation. Mr. Bucher reports that he believed that the parties had agreed to the appointment of a neutral to serve as Administrator. Mr. Shalaby reports that he was ready to suggest appointing John Boessenecker as Administrator as he has a history working with him but that he is willing to discuss the options with Mr. Bucher. Both Messrs. Shalaby and Bucher summarize their client's positions. The court advises the parties that it is apparent from their respective papers that both sides are at an impasse and will continue the matter for both sides to meet and confer regarding appointing a neutral. The court confirms with Mr. Shalaby that the real property belonging to the estate is currently occupied by a family friend, who has advised that they are amenable to moving out of the property on short notice if required.

The matters are continued to Mar-21-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ronald Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Nov-15-2021 Continued To Dec-22-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-15-2021 Continued To Dec-22-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Shalaby, via ZOOM, for Rito Quinonez, Petitioner; Stephen Bucher, via ZOOM, for Guadalupe Martin and Manuela Quinonez, Objectors.

The court asks if the parties might be interested in the appointment of a neutral third party as Administrator. Mr. Shalaby reports that he believes that this matter will not require a neutral party but advises that there is a person willing to serve, if needed. Mr. Bucher reports that this is the first time he's heard of a neutral third party willing to serve as Administrator, but he does believe that pursuant to probate code regarding succession, his client should be appointed as Executor. Mr. Shalaby disagrees and provides oral testimony in support of his position. Mr. Bucher advises that this matter can be resolved at an evidentiary hearing. Mr. Shalaby and Mr. Bucher provide additional testimony in support of their positions. The court confirms the approximate value of the estate with counsel and advises that it is referring the parties to pro bono mediation. The court advises counsel that it typically does not refer cases to mediation this early on, but it appears to the court that the parties will benefit from participating in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than Dec-13-2021. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by Dec-17-2021. The Parties are to return on Dec-22-2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The parties are to monitor the court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the minute order referring the case to pro bono mediation.

The two Petitions for Probate of Will are continued to Dec-22-2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
